name: Varunprasaath R. Selvaraju, Ph.D.
summary: PhD Chemist with 6+ years of experience in process development, analytical characterization, and CMC support for small molecule chemistry. Expertise in process optimization using DOE and statistical methods, analytical method development (HPLC, FT-IR, UV-Vis), and materials characterization (TGA, DSC). Strong background in scaling up chemical processes, establishing process control strategies, and supporting regulatory activities. Proven track record managing multiple development projects and collaborating with cross-functional teams.

education.details:
Dissertation: Modular Design and Electrochemical Properties of Xanthene-Based Electrochromic Materials
Advisor: Prof. Seth Marder

education.degree: B.S. (Research), Major: Chemistry; Minor: Materials Science
education.details: INSPIRE Scholarship for Higher Education (Top 1% nationally)

bullets: Lead chemical process development and optimization for bio-based polymer systems, applying statistical experimental design (DOE) to identify critical process parameters and improve yield.
Establish reproducible process specifications and control strategies for scale-up from lab to pilot quantities.
Develop analytical methods using FT-IR, HPLC, and thermal analysis to monitor process performance and product quality.
Developed synthetic routes and process conditions for organic electrochromic materials, optimizing reaction parameters using DOE methodologies.
Applied analytical characterization (HPLC, FT-IR, UV-Vis, TGA, DSC) to assess process outcomes, purity, and material properties.
Collaborated with cross-functional teams to transfer processes and analytical methods, ensuring reproducibility and quality.
Co-led AFOSR-funded process development for molecular thin-film coatings, developing surface preparation protocols and CVD-based deposition strategies.
Managed process development laboratory operations for 15-member research group; implemented equipment tracking and calibration systems.
Developed SOPs for chemical processes and analytical methods; coordinated resource planning across multiple concurrent projects.

skills.Process Development: Process optimization, DOE, SPC, scale-up support, process control, reaction optimization
skills.Analytical Methods: HPLC, LC-MS, GC-MS, FT-IR, UV-Vis-NIR, TGA, DSC, NMR, method development
skills.CMC & Quality: CMC support, analytical development, technical documentation, SOP development, data integrity
skills.Materials Chemistry: Organic synthesis, polymer chemistry, small molecule characterization, thin-film materials
skills.Project Management: Multi-project coordination, cross-functional collaboration, stakeholder communication

publications: Ramasamy Selvaraju, V.; Venta, R.; Rajapaksha, I.; Zhang, J.; Barlow, S.; Salman, S.; Scott, C.; Marder, S. "Narrow-Band Electrochromism of a Ferrocene-Substituted Rhodamine B." Chemistry of Materials, 2025, 37, 5558–5568.

Mandal, J.; Ramasamy Selvaraju, V.; Yan, W.; Divandari, M.; Spencer, N. D.; Dubner, M. "In Situ Monitoring of SI-ATRP Throughout Multiple Reinitiations Under Flow by Means of a Quartz Crystal Microbalance." RSC Advances, 2018, 8, 20048–20055.

projects.title: Bio-based Polymer Process Development
projects.description: Led chemical process development for novel bio-based polymer systems using statistical experimental design.

projects.title: AFOSR Defense Coatings Program
projects.description: Co-led process development for high-performance molecular thin-film coatings for defense optical applications.
